title: potentilla palustris var. parvifolia
intro: Potentilla palustris var. parvifolia, also known as Dwarf Marsh Cinquefoil, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its small, pale yellow flowers and hairy, deeply lobed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla palustris var. parvifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against various pathogens. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
